description: Turn the current DocOS kernel into a real artifact-driven closed loop with route-registry-orchestrator parsing, deterministic patching, real lint and harness gates, and raw-fixture regression coverage.

userStories.title: Add a unified pipeline run command
userStories.description: As a DocOS operator, I want a single CLI entrypoint to run the whole pipeline so that the system no longer depends on disconnected stage commands.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `docos run <file_path>` or `docos pipeline run <source_id>` command exists and is registered in the CLI help output
The unified command executes ingest, route, parse, normalize, extract, compile, patch, lint, eval, and report in order
If any stage fails, the command stops and reports the failing stage name in CLI output
python -m mypy docos/ exits with code 0
python -m pytest tests/ -q exits with code 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create and persist a RunManifest for each run
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want every pipeline run to create a durable RunManifest so that later stages and reports can trace the run by `run_id`.
acceptanceCriteria: Each unified run creates a real `run_id` and writes a RunManifest artifact to disk
The RunManifest stores `source_id`, stage names, overall status, `started_at`, and `finished_at`
A new process can reload the RunManifest by `run_id` after the original command exits
